Hacker News Confidence

Less annoying hacker news. (What is this?)

This site scrapes Hacker News once every 30 minutes, then sorts according to this formula, with upvotes as positive signals and comments as negative ones. In my experience, the best articles to read on HN are the ones with a high upvote-to-discussion ratio. Mostly because controversial pieces tend to produce a disproportionate number of comments compared to upvotes.


  1. The ANSI art "telecomics" of the 1992 election 51 upvotes, 1 comments
  2. OpenBSD on Motorola 88000 Processors 104 upvotes, 11 comments
  3. I Built an Open-World Engine for the N64 [video] 406 upvotes, 68 comments
  4. Founder of GitLab battles cancer by founding companies 1123 upvotes, 216 comments
  5. A Verilog to Factorio Compiler and Simulator (Working RISC-V CPU) 85 upvotes, 10 comments
  6. CSS is DOOMed 374 upvotes, 85 comments
  7. Linux is an interpreter 210 upvotes, 52 comments
  8. Spanish legislation as a Git repo 754 upvotes, 223 comments
  9. Miasma: A tool to trap AI web scrapers in an endless poison pit 16 upvotes, 1 comments
  10. Cat Itecture: Better Cat Window Boxes (2023) 59 upvotes, 12 comments
  11. OpenCiv1 – open-source rewrite of Civ1 158 upvotes, 45 comments
  12. The Loneliness of a Room of One's Own 26 upvotes, 4 comments
  13. I decompiled the White House's new app 539 upvotes, 198 comments
  14. The Last Contract: William T. Vollmann's Battle to Publish an Epic (2025) 30 upvotes, 6 comments
  15. Monado became the foundation for OpenXR runtimes 12 upvotes, 1 comments
  16. Technology: The (nearly) perfect USB cable tester does exist 92 upvotes, 33 comments
  17. What if AI doesn't need more RAM but better math? 51 upvotes, 19 comments
  18. A laser-based process that enables adhesive-free paper packaging 86 upvotes, 37 comments
  19. Overestimation of microplastics potentially caused by scientists' gloves 33 upvotes, 11 comments
  20. Lat.md: Agent Lattice: a knowledge graph for your codebase, written in Markdown 22 upvotes, 6 comments
  21. I turned my Kindle into my own personal newspaper 53 upvotes, 22 comments
  22. Siclair Microvision (1977) 14 upvotes, 3 comments
  23. Nonfiction Publishing, Under Threat, Is More Important 16 upvotes, 4 comments
  24. Further human + AI + proof assistant work on Knuth's "Claude Cycles" problem 221 upvotes, 147 comments
  25. Alzheimer's disease mortality among taxi and ambulance drivers (2024) 142 upvotes, 93 comments
  26. AI overly affirms users asking for personal advice 667 upvotes, 519 comments
  27. Show HN: Public transit systems as data – lines, stations, railcars, and history 15 upvotes, 6 comments
  28. Android’s new sideload settings will carry over to new devices 104 upvotes, 148 comments
  29. Show HN: Sheet Ninja – Google Sheets as a CRUD Back End for Vibe Coders 10 upvotes, 8 comments
  30. InpharmD (YC W21) Is Hiring – Senior Ruby on Rails Developer 0 upvotes, 0 comments

Last generated 8:00pm, 29 March 2026. Source here.
